task_name: FES (Fundamentals of Endoscopic Surgery) Program Overview - Educational Panel
classification_rationale: This is an educational/lecture presentation about FES curriculum and ABS requirements. It contains slide-based content with speaker (Sara A Hennessy) presenting information about how to prepare residents for FES examination. No actual task performance is shown. No laparoscopic or endoscopic skill execution is visible.
reason_no_score: This video is instructional content about FES requirements, resources, and implementation strategy at UTSW. It does not contain performance of any FLS/MISTELS task. No instruments, no bench model work, no task execution to evaluate.
content_summary: Educational panel discussion on 'Meeting the ABS Mandate - The Program Director's Guide to the FEC Galaxy' by Sara A Hennessy. Topics covered include: definition of FES vs FLS, differences from FLS training, ABS requirements for surgical residents (effective 2018), resources available (testing centers, simulation models), implementation timing, and UTSW-specific implementation strategy using STEP 2 bench models (Truss, MITIE-Operation, Kyoto models) and GI Mentor virtual reality simulation.

educational_topics: FES (Fundamentals of Endoscopic Surgery) definition and relationship to FLS
Differences between FES and FLS training
Simulation resources: inanimate, animate, computer-based models
STEP 2 bench models: Truss, MITIE-Operation, Kyoto
GI Mentor virtual reality simulation for flexible endoscopy
FES curriculum at UT Southwestern: 1-month pretest on STEP models, 1-month dedicated GI endoscopy rotation
resources_mentioned: STEP (Surgical Training for Endoscopic Proficiency) models
Kyoto model with loop reduction and proficiency scoring at 325 seconds
key_takeaways: FES residents cannot train on FLS modules; separate curriculum required
No officially endorsed training protocols exist yet
Clinical experience combined with simulation critical
Pre-test proficiency required before endoscopy rotation
Structured rotation includes didactic + simulation + clinical
